PDK: Process Design Kit — the foundry's description of a manufacturing process: device models, design rules, and standard-cell libraries. LibreLane targets open PDKs like sky130, gf180mcu, and ihp-sg13g2.
SCL: Standard Cell Library — the set of pre-characterized logic gates (NAND, flip-flops, …) the synthesizer maps your RTL onto, with timing/area/power data for each cell.
RTL: Register-Transfer Level — your Verilog/SystemVerilog design described as registers and the logic between them. The input to the flow.
GDSII: The final layout file sent to the foundry — every polygon on every mask layer.
synthesis: Translating RTL into a gate-level netlist of standard cells (done by Yosys).
STA: Static Timing Analysis — checks every path meets timing without simulating, across process corners. Reports setup/hold slack.
WNS: Worst Negative Slack — the most-violated timing path. Negative means timing fails; you need a slower clock or optimization.
TNS: Total Negative Slack — the sum of all negative slacks. A measure of how much total timing work remains.
setup: Setup timing: data must arrive at a flip-flop before the clock edge. Setup violations mean the clock is too fast.
hold: Hold timing: data must stay stable just after the clock edge. Hold violations are fixed by adding delay (buffers).
DRC: Design Rule Check — verifies the layout obeys the foundry's geometric rules (spacing, width, enclosure). Run by Magic and KLayout.
LVS: Layout Versus Schematic — confirms the layout's extracted netlist matches the intended schematic/netlist. Run by Netgen.
antenna: Antenna effect — long metal wires can accumulate charge during fabrication and damage gates. Checked and repaired with diodes.
XOR: Mask XOR — compares two layouts (e.g. Magic vs KLayout GDS) to confirm they're geometrically identical.
EQY: Equivalence checking (Yosys EQY) — formally proves the synthesized gate netlist is logically equivalent to your RTL. Stronger than simulation.
utilization: The fraction of the core area occupied by standard cells. Higher packs tighter (smaller chip) but is harder to route.
PDN: Power Distribution Network — the metal grid that delivers power/ground to every cell.
floorplan: Deciding the die/core size, pin placement, and power grid before placing cells.
placement: Assigning a physical location to every standard cell.
CTS: Clock Tree Synthesis — building a balanced buffer tree so the clock reaches every flip-flop with minimal skew.
routing: Connecting all the cells' pins with metal wires on the available layers.
slack: How much timing margin a path has. Positive = meets timing; negative = violates.
corner: A process/voltage/temperature combination (e.g. slow-slow, fast-fast) timing is verified against.
wirelength: Total length of routed metal. A proxy for area, power, and routability.
DSE: Design-Space Exploration — running the same design under many config/constraint sets to find the best area/timing/power trade-off.
